title: Apples Kept Washing Up. That Was the Problem.
script: Are These Apples Deadly? Tuesday morning, loose apples started washing ashore on the sand in Corolla, North Carolina — hundreds of them, scattered like they'd fallen off a ship. Then came the oranges too. It looked harmless, almost funny, until the Corolla Wild Horse Fund got the call: apples can kill their wild horses, causing choking and deadly colic if the herd finds them first. Local families, deputies, and volunteer turtle patrols dropped everything and raced the clock, bagging up the fruit handful by handful before the horses could. It's not even the strangest thing to wash up here — a few years back it was bags of Doritos. So what nearly poisoned Corolla's wild horses?
